evals.prompt: We want to add multi-tenant support to our SaaS app. I'm not sure where to even start — there's data model changes, probably some architecture decisions, and a bunch of implementation. How should I approach this?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is multi-phase feature work and route along the New Feature golden path, starting with feature-planning, then architecture-design and data-modeling, rather than jumping straight into code.
assertions: Identifies that a dedicated skill / chain applies instead of improvising
Routes to feature-planning as the entry point
Names the relevant downstream skills (architecture-design, data-modeling) as next steps
References the New Feature golden path chain
Hands off to the chosen skill rather than narrating the routing at length

evals.prompt: What's the difference between a stub and a mock?
evals.expected_output: A pure conceptual question with no workflow component. The router should NOT force a skill — it should just answer (optionally noting tdd-workflow/test-suite-design exist if the user later wants to apply it).
assertions: Does NOT invoke a workflow skill for a pure question
Does not over-route or insist on a workflow
At most lightly mentions relevant skills as optional

evals.prompt: Just write me a quick Dockerfile for a Node app — don't bother with any of your skill workflows, I'm in a hurry.
evals.expected_output: User explicitly opted out of the workflow. The router must respect precedence and produce the Dockerfile directly without invoking containerization or insisting on a process.
assertions: Respects the user's explicit opt-out (does not force the containerization skill)
Does not lecture about following the workflow
Treats user instructions as taking precedence over the soft routing rule
